creator: Rebecca Bellan, Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Connie Loizos
content: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.
contentSnippet: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

title: (No AI) Nvidia says its AI data center design runs hotter to use a lot less water
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![Graphic image of a data center.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/STKS528_DATA_CENTERS2_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Public pushback against data centers has emphasized their [water and energy consumption](https://www.theverge.com/news/845831/ai-chips-data-center-power-water), and now Nvidia is [highlighting](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/liquid-cooling-ai-factories/) its claim that the Rubin generation reference design for a fully liquid-cooled data center has "eliminated massive amounts of power usage and pretty much all water usage." Still, it doesn't address all of the concerns around AI data centers, including during their construction, and for the [power generation requirements](https://www.theverge.com/news/870422/data-center-ai-gas-power-surge) of the massive facilities. Also, as [_Gizmodo_](https://gizmodo.com/nvidia-touts-100-reduction-in-water-use-with-new-data-center-design-2000775354) points out, Nvidia's blog post doesn't mention the cost of building this style of data center vs. one using less efficient air cooling, but claims that "every cloud …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/954139/nvidia-data-centers-rubin-liquid-cooling)
contentSnippet:
Public pushback against data centers has emphasized their water and energy consumption, and now Nvidia is highlighting its claim that the Rubin generation reference design for a fully liquid-cooled data center has "eliminated massive amounts of power usage and pretty much all water usage." Still, it doesn't address all of the concerns around AI data centers, including during their construction, and for the power generation requirements of the massive facilities. Also, as Gizmodo points out, Nvidia's blog post doesn't mention the cost of building this style of data center vs. one using less efficient air cooling, but claims that "every cloud  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Public pushback against data centers has emphasized their water and energy consumption, and now Nvidia is highlighting its claim that the Rubin generation reference design for a fully liquid-cooled data center has "eliminated massive amounts of power usage and pretty much all water usage." Still, it doesn't address all of the concerns around AI data […]

title: (No AI) AI is cursing renters with the promise of impossible homes
author: Gaby Del Valle
content:
![Photo collage of images that use virtual staging.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/268589_Vitual_staging_AParkin.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Joyce, a native New Yorker, didn't think finding her first solo apartment in the city would be easy. But she also didn't think it'd be "hell." After looking at a lot of tiny, overpriced places she described as "shitholes," Joyce found her dream apartment: a reasonably priced studio in Manhattan.

"It was big and airy, and there was a fireplace," she said. The kitchen was small but well equipped and looked like it had been recently renovated. She dropped everything to see the apartment, and when she got there, she learned that five other women, all around her age, had viewings scheduled after hers.

"I get in, and it's not the same apartment …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/report/953888/ai-virtual-staging-real-estate-apartment-listings)

title: (No AI) Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
content: OpenAI introduces Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative helping open-source maintainers find, validate, and fix vulnerabilities with AI and expert review.
contentSnippet: OpenAI introduces Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative helping open-source maintainers find, validate, and fix vulnerabilities with AI and expert review.

title: (No AI) Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
content: OpenAI introduces new Daybreak tools, including Codex Security and GPT-5.5-Cyber, to help organizations find, validate, and patch vulnerabilities at scale.
contentSnippet: OpenAI introduces new Daybreak tools, including Codex Security and GPT-5.5-Cyber, to help organizations find, validate, and patch vulnerabilities at scale.
